When a guest posts a review I immediately receive an email and text so that I can respond in real time.

review_title: I wish I never chose this company for marketing
description: My experience working with Hibu was extremely disappointing and ultimately a waste of my time, money, and effort. I was initially contacted and encouraged to switch from my existing website to one they would provide, which was presented to me as a free, optimized solution that would improve my business visibility. However, after signing, I later learned from another representative that I was actually being charged for the website in addition to the marketing service all along. Throughout our agreement, I raised repeated concerns that my business was being advertised in the wrong city. Despite paying monthly, my concerns went unanswered, and when leads did come in, potential customers believed my business was located in the incorrect location listed in the ads. As a result, I did not gain any new clients from the service I was paying for. After four months, I requested to end the agreement. Although multiple representatives acknowledged that the ads were running in the wrong area, each person passed responsibility to someone else and claimed they didn’t understand how the mistake happened. I was told the issue would be escalated and corrected, but it never was. Instead, billing continued and payment was still demanded, with little interest shown in actually resolving the problem or providing satisfactory service. Even my sales representative, [sensitive content hidden], contacted me stating he would help resolve the situation, but after that conversation I received no follow-up or assistance. Overall, my experience was frustrating, misleading, and unproductive.
author: Monique J.
pros: In person assigned sales rep unfortunately this didn’t help because he only emailed supposed results a few times a month. When I sent him messages asking him to correct errors I often didn’t receive a response.
cons: Seemed more interested in locking into a contract and receiving payment than producing results and being accurate in their marketing for the company.
